title: Manually Creating and Deleting Uniface Router and Server Accounts
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on Microsoft Windows
Manually Creating and Deleting Uniface Router and Server Accounts
text: Manually Creating and Deleting Uniface Router and Server Accounts If the installer was unable to create accounts for the Uniface Server and Uniface Router on Microsoft Windows, you can manually create these accounts. Account creation may fail, for example, if the account already exists with a different password, or if your system required passwords to conform to a specific pattern or length. Even if the accounts were created, you may want to change the passwords to something that is more secure. In this case, you need to delete the accounts and recreate them with the new password. Open a command prompt and navigate to the common/bin directory of your Uniface installation. For example: cd /Uniface10/common/bin If you are changing the password of an existing account, run the urouter.exe executable with the /delacc switch. For example, to delete the default Uniface Server account: Enterprise Edition: urouter/delacc userver Community Edition: urouter/delacc UnifaceServer Run the urouter.exe executable with the /newacc switch and optional parameters. For example: urouter/newacc userver /passwd my2userv&c Copy If you have specified a different password for the Uniface Server account, modify the associated password in web.xml , and in any assignment files where it is specified. For example, in web.xml , edit the MIDDLEWARE parameter: <init-param> <param-name>MIDDLEWARE</param-name> <param-value>UV8:localhost+13003|userver|my2userv&c|wasv</param-value> </init-param> Copy Related Links /delacc Delete a Uniface Router or Uniface Server account on Windows platforms. /newacc Create a new account on Windows platforms for the Uniface Router or Uniface Server. /passwd Creates a password for the newly created Uniface Server or Uniface Router account.
